title: Context Vector Calculation
concept: The context vector is a weighted sum of all encoder hidden states, updated dynamically at each decoder step to focus on the target word.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of the context vector in translation models?

options.text: To store the final output translation
To summarize the relevant parts of the input for the decoder
To erase unwanted words from the source sentence

exercises.explain: The context vector acts as a dynamic bridge, passing focused information from the encoder (input reader) to the decoder (output writer).

cards.title: Focus on What Matters
cards.body: How does an AI translator know which words to focus on when writing a sentence? It uses a dynamic helper called the 'context vector' to package up the most important clues at just the right moment!

cards.title: What is a Context Vector?
cards.body: The context vector is a dynamic summary representation. It is calculated by taking a weighted sum of all the encoder's hidden states, where the weights represent how much attention the decoder should pay to each input word at the current step.

cards.title: The Spotlight Analogy
cards.body: Imagine you are a detective examining a wall of clues (encoder states) to write your case report. Instead of reading all clues with equal interest, you shine a spotlight on the clues most relevant to the paragraph you are writing right now. The spotlighted area is your context vector!

cards.title: Translation in Action
cards.body: When translating 'The black cat' to French ('Le chat noir'), when the decoder is about to output 'chat' (cat), the context vector dynamically calculates high weights for 'cat', creating a vector that highly represents the concept of the feline.

cards.title: Myth: One Size Fits All
cards.body:
Myth: The context vector is a fixed summary of the input sentence that never changes.
Reality: The context vector is dynamic! It is recalculating and shifting its focus at every single step of the output generation.

cards.title: Static vs. Dynamic Context
cards.body: Let's compare a static approach (used in older models) with the dynamic context vector approach.

left.text: Tries to squeeze the entire input sentence into one fixed-size vector for the whole translation. Details get lost!

right.text: Updates at every step, creating a custom spotlight on the relevant encoder states for each word.

exercises.prompt: What is the context vector mathematically composed of?

options.text: A weighted sum of all encoder hidden states
A simple average of all decoder outputs
The final encoder hidden state only
A random selection of input words

exercises.explain: The context vector is calculated as a weighted sum of all the encoder's hidden states, allowing the model to focus on different parts of the input sequence.

exercises.prompt: The context vector is updated dynamically at each decoder step to focus on the target word.
exercises.explain: True. The context vector is updated at each step of the decoder to ensure the model focuses on the most relevant input words for the current output word.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how the context vector combines encoder states.
exercises.sentence: The context vector is a sum of all encoder hidden states based on attention scores.
exercises.explain: The context vector is a weighted sum, meaning different parts of the input are given different levels of importance (weights) based on what the decoder is doing.

exercises.prompt: Which AI model correctly explains how the context vector behaves during translation?

options.text: AI Model A says: 'The context vector is computed once at the beginning and remains identical for every output word.'
AI Model B says: 'The context vector is recalculated at every step to focus on different parts of the input.'
AI Model C says: 'The context vector only changes when translating verbs, but stays the same for nouns.'

exercises.explain: AI Model B is correct because the context vector changes at each step of the decoding process to focus on different input words as the translation progress. It is not static.

exercises.prompt: When and how often is the context vector updated during the decoding process?

options.text: It is calculated once at the start and stays the same
It is dynamically re-calculated at every single decoder step
It is only updated when the model encounters punctuation
It is ignored after the first output word is generated

exercises.explain: The context vector is not static; it is re-calculated at every single step of the decoder. This allows the model to shift its focus to different parts of the input sequence as it generates each new word in the output sequence.
